author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter IV. 1–18.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It ought not to be a matter of wonder that a miracle was wrought by God; the wonder would be if man had wrought it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Rather ought we to rejoice than wonder that our Lord and Saviour Jesus Christ was made man, than that He performed divine works among men.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is of greater importance to our salvation what He was made for men, than what He did among men: it is more important that He healed the faults of souls, than that He healed the weaknesses of mortal bodies.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But as the soul knew not Him by whom it was to be healed, and had eyes in the flesh whereby to see corporeal deeds, but had not yet sound eyes in the heart with which to recognise Him as God concealed in the flesh, He wrought what the soul was able to see, in order to heal that by which it was not able to see.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He entered a place where lay a great multitude of sick folk—of blind, lame, withered; and being the physician both of souls and bodies, and having come to heal all the souls of them that should believe, of those sick folk He chose one for healing, thereby to signify unity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If in doing this we regard Him with a commonplace mind, with the mere human understanding and wit, as regards power it was not a great matter that He performed; and also as regards goodness He performed too little.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There lay so many there, and yet only one was healed, whilst He could by a word have raised them all up.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What, then, must we understand but that the power and the goodness was doing what souls might, by His deeds, understand for their everlasting salvation, than what bodies might gain for temporal health?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For that which is the real health of bodies, and which is looked for from the Lord, will be at the end, in the resurrection of the dead.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What shall live then shall no more die; what shall be healed shall no more be sick; what shall be satisfied shall no more hunger and thirst; what shall be made new shall not grow old.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But at this time, however, the eyes of the blind, that were opened by those acts of our Lord and Saviour Jesus Christ, were again closed in death; and limbs of the paralytics that received strength were loosened again in death; and whatever was for a time made whole in mortal limbs came to nought in the end: but the soul that believed passed to eternal life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly, to the soul that should believe, whose sins He had come to forgive, to the healing of whose ailments He had humbled Himself, He gave a significant proof by the healing of this impotent man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of the profound mystery of this thing and this proof, so far as the Lord deigns to grant us, while you are attentive and aiding our weakness by prayer, I will speak as I shall have ability.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And whatever I am not able to do, that will be supplied to you by Him by whose help I do what I can.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of this pool, which was surrounded with five porches, in which lay a great multitude of sick folk, I remember that I have very often treated; and most of you will with me recollect what I am about to say, rather than gain the knowledge of it for the first time.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But it is by no means unprofitable to go back upon matters already known, that both they who know not may be instructed, and they who do know may be confirmed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, as being already known, these things must be touched upon briefly, not leisurely inculcated.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That pool and that water seem to me to have signified the Jewish people.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For that peoples are signified under the name of waters the Apocalypse of John clearly indicates to us, where, after he had been shown many waters, and he had asked what they were, was answered that they were peoples.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That water, then—namely, that people—was shut in by the five books of Moses, as by five porches.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But those books brought forth the sick, not healed them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the law convicted, not acquitted sinners.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly the letter, without grace, made men guilty, whom on confessing grace delivered.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For this is what the apostle saith: “For if a law had been given which could have given life, verily righteousness should have been by the law.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why, then, was the law given?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He goes on to say, “But the Scripture hath concluded all under sin, that the promise by faith of Jesus Christ might be given to them that believe.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What more evident?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Have not these words expounded to us both the five porches, and also the multitude of sick folk?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The five porches are the law.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why did not the five porches heal the sick folk?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because, “if there had been a law given which could have given life, verily righteousness should have been by the law.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why, then, did the porches contain those whom they did not heal?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because “the Scripture hath concluded all under sin, that the promise by faith of Jesus Christ might be given to them that believe.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What was done, then, that they who could not be healed in the porches might be healed in that water after being troubled?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For on a sudden the water was seen troubled, and that by which it was troubled was not seen.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou mayest believe that this was wont to be done by angelic virtue, yet not without some mystery being implied.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: After the water was troubled, the one who was able cast himself in, and he alone was healed: whoever went in after that one, did so in vain.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What, then, is meant by this, unless it be that there came one, even Christ, to the Jewish people; and by doing great things, by teaching profitable things, troubled sinners, troubled the water by His presence, and roused it towards His own death?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But He was hidden that troubled.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For had they known Him, they would never have crucified the Lord of glory.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore, to go down into the troubled water means to believe in the Lord’s death.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There only one was healed, signifying unity: whoever came thereafter was not healed, because whoever shall be outside unity cannot be healed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now let us see what He intended to signify in the case of that one whom He Himself, keeping the mystery of unity, as I said before, deigned to heal out of so many sick folk.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He found in the number of this man’s years the number, so to speak, of infirmity: “He was thirty and eight years in infirmity.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How this number refers more to weakness than to health must be somewhat more carefully expounded.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I wish you to be attentive; the Lord will aid us, so that I may fitly speak, and that you may sufficiently hear.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The number forty is commended to our attention as one consecrated by a kind of perfection.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This, I suppose, is well known to you, beloved.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Holy Scriptures very often testify to the fact.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Fasting was consecrated by this number, as you are well aware.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For Moses fasted forty days, and Elias as many; and our Lord and Saviour Jesus Christ did Himself fulfill this number of fasting.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By Moses is signified the law; by Elias, the prophets; by the Lord, the gospel.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It was for this reason that these three appeared on that mountain, where He showed Himself to His disciples in the brightness of His countenance and vesture.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He appeared in the middle, between Moses and Elias, as the gospel had witness from the law and the prophets.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whether, therefore, in the law, or in the prophets, or in the gospel, the number forty is commended to our attention in the case of fasting.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now fasting, in its large and general sense, is to abstain from the iniquities and unlawful pleasures of the world, which is perfect fasting: “That, denying ungodliness and worldly lusts, we may live temperately, and righteously, and godly in this present world.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What reward does the apostle join to this fast?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He goes on to say: “Looking for that blessed hope, and the appearing of the glory of the blessed God, and our Saviour Jesus Christ.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In this world, then, we celebrate, as it were, the forty days’ abstinence, when we live aright, and abstain from iniquities and from unlawful pleasures.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But because this abstinence shall not be without reward, we look for “that blessed hope, and the revelation of the glory of the great God, and of our Saviour Jesus Christ.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In that hope, when the reality of the hope shall have come to pass, we shall receive our wages, a penny (denarius).

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the same is the wages given to the workers laboring in the vineyard, as I presume you remember; for we are not to repeat everything, as if to persons wholly ignorant and inexperienced.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A denarius, then, which takes its name from the number ten, is given, and this joined with the forty makes up fifty; whence it is that before Easter we keep the Quadragesima with labor, but after Easter we keep the Quinquagesima with joy, as having received our wages.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now to this, as if to the wholesome labor of a good work, which belongs to the number forty, there is added the denarius of rest and happiness, that it may be made the number fifty.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord Jesus Himself showed this also far more openly, when He companied on earth with His disciples during forty days after His resurrection; and having on the fortieth day ascended into heaven, did at the end of ten days send the wages, the Holy Ghost.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: These were done in signs, and by a kind of signs were the very realities anticipated.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By significant tokens are we fed, that we may be able to come to the enduring realities.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We are workmen, and are still laboring in the vineyard: when the day is ended and the work finished, the wages will be paid.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what workman can hold out to the receiving of the wages, unless he be fed while he labors?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Even thou thyself wilt not give thy workman only wages; wilt thou not also bestow on him that where with he may repair his strength in his labor?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Surely thou feedest him to whom thou art to give wages.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In like manner also doth the Lord, in those significant tokens of the Scriptures, feed us while we labor.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if that joy in understanding holy mysteries be withdrawn from us, we faint in labor, and there will be none to come to the reward.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How, then, is work perfected in the number forty?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The reason, it may be, is, because the law was given in ten precepts, and was to be preached throughout the whole world: which whole world, we are to mark, is made up of four quarters, east and west, south and north, whence the number ten, multiplied by four, comes to forty.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Or, it may be, because the law is fulfilled by the gospel, which has four books: for in the gospel it is said, “I came not to destroy the law, but to fulfill it.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whether, then, it be for this reason or for that, or for some other more probable, which is hid from us, but not from more learned men; certain it is, however, that in the number forty a certain perfection in good works is signified, which good works are most of all practised by a kind of abstinence from unlawful lusts of the world, that is, by fasting in the general sense.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear also the apostle when he says, “Love is the fulfilling of the law.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whence the love?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By the grace of God, by the Holy Spirit.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For we could not have it from ourselves, as if making it for ourselves.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is the gift of God, and a great gift it is: for, saith he, “the love of God is shed abroad in our hearts by the Holy Spirit, which is given to us.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore love completes the law, and most truly it is said, “Love is the perfecting of the law.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us inquire as to this love, in what manner the Lord doth commend it to our consideration.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Remember what I laid down: I want to explain the number thirty-eight of the years of that impotent man, why that number thirty-eight is one of weakness rather than of health.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now, as I was saying, love fulfills the law.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The number forty belongs to the perfecting of the law in all works; but in love two precepts are committed to our keeping.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Keep before your eyes, I beseech you, and fix in your memory, what I say; be ye not despisers of the word, that your soul may not become a trodden path, where the seed cast cannot sprout, “and the fowls of the air will come and gather it up.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Apprehend it, and lay it up in your hearts.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The precepts of love, given to us by the Lord, are two: “Thou shalt love the Lord thy God with all thy heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy mind;” and, “Thou shalt love thy neighbor as thyself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: On these two commandments hang all the law and the prophets.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: With good reason did the widow cast “two mites,” all her substance, into the offerings of God: with good reason did the host take “two” pieces of money, for the poor man that was wounded by the robbers, for his making whole: with good reason did Jesus spent two days with the Samaritans, to establish them in love.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thus, whilst a certain good thing is generally signified by this number two, most especially is love in its twofold character set forth to us thereby.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, therefore, the number forty possesses the perfecting of the law, and the law is fulfilled only in the twin precepts of love, why dost thou wonder that he was weak and sick, who was short of forty by two?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore let us now see the sacred mystery whereby this impotent man is healed by the Lord.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord Himself came, the Teacher of love, full of love, “shortening,” as it was predicted of Him, “the word upon the earth,” and showed that the law and the prophets hang on two precepts of love.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Upon these hung Moses with his number forty, upon these Elias with his; and the Lord brought in this number in His testimony.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This impotent man is healed by the Lord in person; but before healing him, what does He say to him?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Wilt thou be made whole?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The man answered that he had not a man to put him into the pool.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Truly he had need of a “man” to his healing, but that “man” one who is also God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For there is one God, and one Mediator between God and man, the man Christ Jesus.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He came, then, the Man who was needed: why should the healing be delayed?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Arise,” saith He; “take up thy bed, and walk.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He said three things: “Arise, Take up thy bed, and Walk.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But that “Arise” was not a command to do a work, but the operation of healing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And the man, on being made whole, received two commands: “Take up thy bed, and Walk.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I ask you, why was it not enough to say, “Walk?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Or, at any rate, why was it not enough to say, “Arise”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For when the man had arisen whole, he would not have remained in the place.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Would it not be for the purpose of going away that he would have arisen?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: My impression is, that He who found the man lacking two things, gave him these two precepts: for, by ordering him to do two things, it is as if He filled up that which was lacking.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How, then, do we find the two precepts of love indicated in these two commands of the Lord?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Take up thy bed,” saith He, “and walk.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What the two precepts are, my brethren, recollect with me.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For they ought to be thoroughly familiar to you, and not merely to come into your mind when they are recited by us, but they ought never to be blotted out from your hearts.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let it ever be your supreme thought, that you must love God and your neighbor: “God with all thy heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy mind; and thy neighbor as thyself.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: These must always be pondered, meditated, retained, practised, and fulfilled.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The love of God comes first in the order of enjoying; but in the order of doing, the love of our neighbor comes first.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He who commanded thee this love in two precepts did not charge thee to love thy neighbor first, and then God, but first God, afterwards thy neighbor.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou however, as thou dost not yet see God dost earn to see Him by loving thy neighbor; by loving thy neighbor thou purgest thine eye for seeing God, as John evidently says, “If thou lovest not thy brother whom thou seest, how canst thou love God, whom thou dost not see?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See, thou art told, “Love God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If thou say to me, “Show me Him, that I may love Him;” what shall I answer, but what the same John saith: “No man hath seen God at any time”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And, that you may not suppose yourself to be wholly estranged from seeing God, he saith, “God is love; and he that dwelleth in love dwelleth in God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore love thy neighbor; look at the source of thy love of thy neighbor; there thou wilt see, as thou mayest, God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Begin, then, to love thy neighbor.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Break thy bread to the hungry, and bring into thy house him that is needy without shelter; if thou seest the naked, clothe him; and despise not those of the household of thy seed.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And in doing this, what wilt thou get in consequence?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Then shall thy light break forth as the morning light.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thy light is thy God, a “morning light” to thee, because He shall come to thee after the night of this world: for He neither rises nor sets, because He is ever abiding.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He will be a morning light to thee on thy return, He who had set for thee on thy falling away from Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, in this “Take up thy bed,” He seems to me to have said, Love thy neighbor.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But why the love of our neighbor is set forth by the taking up of the bed, is still shut up, and, as I suppose, needs to be expounded: unless, perhaps, it offend us that our neighbor should be indicated by means of a bed, a stolid, senseless thing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let not my neighbor be angry if he be set forth to us by a thing without soul and without feeling.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord Himself, even our Saviour Jesus Christ, is called the corner-stone, to build up two in Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He is called also a rock, from which water flowed forth: “And that rock was Christ.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What wonder, then, if Christ is called rock, that neighbor is called wood?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yet not any kind of wood whatever; as neither that was any kind of rock soever, but one from which water flowed to the thirsty; nor any kind soever of stone, but a corner-stone, which in itself coupled two walls coming from different directions.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So neither mayest thou take thy neighbor to be wood of any kind soever, but a bed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then what is there in a bed, pray?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What, but that the impotent man was borne on it; but, when made whole, he carries the bed?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What does the apostle say?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Bear ye one another’s burdens, and so shall ye fulfill the law of Christ.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now the law of Christ is love, and love is not fulfilled except we bear one another’s burdens.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Forbearing,” saith he, “one another in love, endeavoring to keep the unity of the Spirit in the bond of peace.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When thou wast weak thy neighbor bore thee: thou art made whole, bear thy neighbor.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So wilt thou fill up, O man, that which was lacking to thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Take up thy bed, then.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But when thou hast taken it up, stay not in the place; “walk.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By loving thy neighbor, by caring for thy neighbor, dost thou perform thy going.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whither goest thy way, but to the Lord God, whom we ought to love with the whole heart, and with the whole soul, and with the whole mind?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For we are not yet come to the Lord, but we have our neighbor with us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Bear him, then, when thou walkest, that thou mayest come to Him with whom thou desirest to abide.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, “take up thy bed, and walk.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The man did this, and the Jews were offended.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For they saw a man carrying his bed on the Sabbath-day, and they did not blame the Lord for healing him on the Sabbath, that He should be able to answer them, that if any of them had a beast fallen into a well, he would surely draw it out on the Sabbath-day, and save his beast; and so, now they did not object to Him that a man was made whole on the Sabbath-day, but that the man was carrying his bed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if the healing was not to be deferred, should a work also have been commanded?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “It is not lawful for thee,” say they, to do what thou art doing, “to take up thy bed.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And he, in defence, put the author of his healing before his censors, saying, “He that made me whole, the same said unto me, Take up thy bed, and walk.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Should I not take injunction from him from whom I received healing?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And they said, “Who is the man that said unto thee, Take up thy bed, and walk?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “But he that was made whole knew not who it was” that had said this to him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For Jesus,” when He had done this, and given him this order, “turned away from him in the crowd.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See how this also is fulfilled.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We bear our neighbor, and walk towards God; but Him, to whom we are walking, we do not yet see: for that reason also, that man did not yet know Jesus.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The mystery herein intimated to us is, that we believe on Him whom we do not yet see; and that He may not be seen, He turns aside in the crowd.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is difficult in a crowd to see Christ: a certain solitude is necessary for our mind; it is by a certain solitude of contemplation that God is seen.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A crowd has noise; this seeing requires secrecy.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Take up thy bed”—being thyself borne, bear thy neighbor; “and walk,” that thou mayest come to the goal.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do not seek Christ in a crowd: He is not as one of a crowd; He excels all crowd.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That great fish first ascended from the sea, and He sits in heaven making intercession for us: as the great high priest He entered alone into that within the veil; the crowd stands without.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do thou walk, bearing thy neighbor: if thou hast learned to bear, thou, who wast wont to be borne.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In a word, even now as yet thou knowest not Jesus, not yet seest Jesus: what follows thereafter?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Since that man desisted not from taking up his bed and walking, “Jesus seeth him afterwards in the temple.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He did not see Jesus in the crowd, he saw Him in the temple.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord Jesus, indeed, saw him both in the crowd and in the temple; but the impotent man does not know Jesus in the crowd, but he knows Him in the temple.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The man came then to the Lord: saw Him in the temple, saw Him in a consecrated, saw Him in a holy place.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what does the Lord say to him?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Behold, thou art made whole; sin no more, lest some worse thing befall thee.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The man, then, after he saw Jesus, and knew Him to be the author of his healing, was not slothful in preaching Him whom he had seen: “He departed, and told the Jews that it was Jesus that had made him whole.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He brought them word, and they were mad against him; he preached his own salvation, they sought not their own salvation.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Jews persecuted the Lord Jesus because He did these things on the Sabbath-day.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us hear what answer the Lord now made to the Jews.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I have told you how He is wont to answer concerning the healing of men on the Sabbath-day, that they used not on the Sabbath-day to slight their cattle, either in delivering or in feeding them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What does He answer concerning the carrying of the bed?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A manifest corporal work was done before the eyes of the Jews; not a healing of the body, but a bodily work, which appeared not so necessary as the healing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let the Lord, then, openly declare that the sacrament of the Sabbath, even the sign of keeping one day, was given to the Jews for a time, but that the fulfillment of the sacrament had come in Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “My Father,” saith He, “worketh hitherto, and I work.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He sent a great commotion among them: the water is troubled by the coming of the Lord, but yet He that troubles is not seen.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yet one great sick one is to be healed by the troubled water, the whole world by the death of the Lord.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us see, then, the answer made by the Truth: “My Father worketh hitherto, and I work.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is it false, then, which the Scripture has said, that “God rested from all His works on the seventh day”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And does the Lord Jesus speak contrary to this Scripture ministered by Moses, whilst He Himself says to the Jews, “If ye believed Moses, ye would believe me; for He wrote of me”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See, then, whether Moses did not mean it to be significant of something that “God rested on the seventh day.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For God had not become wearied in doing the work of His own creation, and needed rest as a man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How can He have been wearied, who made by a word?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yet is both that true, that “God rested from His works on the seventh day;” and this also is true that Jesus saith, “My Father worketh hitherto.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But who can unfold it in words, man to men, weak to weak, unlearned to them that seek to learn; and if he chance to understand somewhat, unable to bring it forth and unfold it to men, who with difficulty, it may be, receive it, even if what is received can possibly be unfolded?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who, I say, my brethren, can unfold in words how God both works while at rest, and rests while working?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I pray you to put this matter off while you are advancing on the way; for this seeing requires the temple of God, requires the holy place.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Bear your neighbor, and walk.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ye shall see Him in that place where ye shall not require the words of men.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Perhaps we can more appropriately say this, that in the saying, “God rested on the seventh day,” he signified by a great mystery the Lord and our Saviour Jesus Christ Himself, who spoke and said, “My Father worketh hitherto, and I work.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Lord Jesus is, of course, God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He is the Word of God, and you have heard that “in the begin ning was the Word;” and not any word whatsoever, but “the Word was God, and all things were made by Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He was perhaps signified as about to rest on the seventh day from all His works.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For, read the Gospel, and see what great works Jesus wrought.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He wrought our salvation on the cross, that all things foretold by the prophets might be fulfilled in Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He was crowned with thorns; He hung on the tree; said, “I thirst,” received vinegar on a sponge, that it might be fulfilled which was said, “And in my thirst they gave me vinegar to drink.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And when all His works were completed, on the sixth day of the week, He bowed His head and gave up the ghost, and on the Sabbath-day He rested in the tomb from all His works.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore it is as if He said to the Jews, “Why do ye expect that I should not work on the Sabbath?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Sabbath-day was ordained for you for a sign of me.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You observe the works of God: I was there when they were made, by me were they all made; I know them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: ‘My Father worketh hitherto.’

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Father made the light, but He spoke that there should be light; if He spoke, it was by His Word He made it: His Word I was, I am; by me was the world made in those works, by me the world is ruled in these works.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: My Father worked when He made the world, and hitherto now worketh while He rules the world: therefore by me He made when He made, and by me He rules while He rules.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This He said, but to whom?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To men deaf, blind, lame, impotent, not acknowledging the physician, and as if in a frenzy they had lost their wits, wishing to slay Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Further, what said the evangelist as he went on?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Therefore the Jews sought the more to kill Him, because He not only broke the Sabbath, but said also that God was His Father;” not in any ordinary manner, but how?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Making Himself equal with God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For we all say to God, “Our Father which art in heaven;” we read also that the Jews said, “Seeing Thou art our Father.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore it was not for this they were angry, because He said that God was His Father, but because He said it in quite another way than men do.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, the Jews understand what the Arians do not understand.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Arians, in fact, say that the Son is not equal with the Father, and hence it is that the heresy was driven from the Church.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Lo, the very blind, the very slayers of Christ, still understood the words of Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They did not understand Him to be Christ, nor did they understand Him to be the Son of God: but they did nevertheless understand that in these words such a Son of God was intimated to them as should be equal with God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who He was they knew not; still they did acknowledge such a One to be declared, in that “He said God was His Father, making Himself equal with God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Was He not therefore equal with God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He did not make Himself equal, but the Father begat Him equal.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Were He to make Himself equal, He would fall by robbery.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For he who wished to make himself equal with God, whilst he was not so, fell, and of an angel became a devil, and administered to man that cup of pride by which himself was cast down.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For this fallen said to man, envying his standing, “Taste, and ye shall be as gods;” that is, seize to yourselves by usurpation that which ye are not made, for I also have been cast down by robbery.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He did not put forth this, but this is what he persuaded to.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Christ, however, was begotten equal to the Father, not made; begotten of the substance of the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whence the apostle thus declares Him: “Who, being in the form of God, thought it not robbery to be equal with God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What means “thought it not robbery”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He usurped not equality with God, but was in that equality in which He was begotten.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And how were we to come to the equal God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He emptied Himself, taking upon Him the form of a servant.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But He emptied Himself not by losing what He was, but by taking to Him what He was not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Jews, despising this form of a servant, could not understand the Lord Christ equal to the Father, although they had not the least doubt that He affirmed this of Himself, and therefore were they enraged: and yet He still bore with them, and sought the healing of them, while they raged against Him.
